findings.evidence: Line 69 `@[simp, grind =] theorem isCharacteristicVector_iff ... := Iff.rfl` unfolds the head; line 88 `@[simp, grind .] theorem isCharacteristicVector_canonicalCharacteristic : P.IsCharacteristicVector P.canonicalCharacteristic` keys on that same head.
findings.fix: Pick one driver: drop `@[simp]` from `isCharacteristicVector_iff` (keep it as a `_def`-style restatement, optionally `@[grind =]`) and let the per-instance/algebraic lemmas drive simp; or, if the predicate is meant to always unfold, drop `@[simp]` from `isCharacteristicVector_canonicalCharacteristic`.
findings.issue: `isCharacteristicVector_iff` is `@[simp, grind =]`, so simp unfolds `P.IsCharacteristicVector k` to `∀ v, k v ≡ P.weight v [ZMOD 2]` for all `k`. This makes the also-`@[simp]` lemma `isCharacteristicVector_canonicalCharacteristic` (line 88) dead, since the head it matches is dissolved before it can fire; the same will kill any future per-instance characteristic simp lemma.

summary: Surface is otherwise minimal and the prior abbrev/body exposure is fixed, but the simp/grind annotations conflict: marking the definitional-unfold lemma `isCharacteristicVector_iff` as `@[simp]` rewrites the `IsCharacteristicVector` head away, leaving the `@[simp]` canonical-instance lemma (and any future per-instance simp lemmas) unable to fire.
